name: Champagne Celebration Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease and lightly flour one 9-inch round pan and one 13 x 9 x 2 inch pan; set aside.
2. In a very large bowl, stir together flour, sugar, baking powder, soda, and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Add milk, shortening, 1/2 cup champagne, vanilla, and, if desired, red food coloring. Beat on low to medium speed about 30 seconds or just until combined, scraping the bowl constantly. Beat on medium speed for 2 minutes.
3. Add beaten egg whites; beat an additional 2 minutes. (The batter may appear slightly curdled.)
4. Spread 2-3/4 cups of batter in the 9-inch pan and the remaining batter in the 13 x 9 x 2 inch pan.
5. Bake at 350°F for 30-35 minutes for the round cake, and 40-45 minutes for the rectangular cake, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Cool cakes in pans on racks for 10 minutes. Remove from pans and cool completely.
6. Using cardboard circle patterns of 4 and 6 inches, cut one 4-inch and one 6-inch cake layer from the 13 x 9 x 2 inch cake. If desired, drizzle 3 tablespoons of champagne over the top of each round cake layer.
7. Place the 9-inch cake layer on a serving plate; frost sides and top with remaining frosting. Lining up the backs of the layers, place the 6-inch cake on top of the 9-inch cake; frost sides and top. Repeat with the remaining 4-inch cake layer.
8. If desired, tint any remaining frosting; decorate with tinted frosting and edible flowers. Serves 24.
Champagne Buttercream Frosting:
In a bowl, beat shortening and butter or margarine until combined. Beat in champagne or cream sherry. Beat in sifted powdered sugar and a few drops of red food coloring. If necessary, beat in additional champagne or cream sherry to make frosting spreading consistency. Makes 3-3/4 cups.
